step_rationale: Early AMI diagnosis biomarker; cardiac injury marker in ischemia-reperfusion; endothelial injury mediator.
mechanism: miR-499, cardiac-specific microRNA, is released early in myocardial ischemia and necrosis; elevated levels indicate cardiomyocyte damage and endothelial injury.

references.title: Advancements in Biomarkers for Early Detection and Risk Stratification of Cardiovascular Diseases-A Literature Review.
Human Heart Anoxia and Reperfusion Tissue (HEART) Model for the Rapid Study of Exosome Bound miRNA Expression As Biomarkers for Myocardial Infarction.
Copeptin, miRNA-208, and miRNA-499 as New Biomarkers for Early Detection of Acute Coronary Syndrome.
miR-499 released during myocardial infarction causes endothelial injury by targeting α7-nAchR.
references.journal: Journal of cellular and molecular medicine

references.title: Serum miRNA-499 and miRNA-210: A potential role in early diagnosis of acute coronary syndrome.
Circulating microRNA: a novel potential biomarker for early diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in humans.
